sentence: וַיִּקְרָא אֱלֹהִים לָאוֹר יוֹם וְלַחֹשֶׁךְ קָרָא לָיְלָה וַיְהִי־עֶרֶב וַיְהִי־בֹקֶר יוֹם אֶחָד

grammatical-notes.וַיִּקְרָא: Conjunction 'va' (waw-consecutive) + Verb 'yiqra' (Qal Imperfect 3ms). Sequential act of naming and defining.
grammatical-notes.קָרָא: Verb, Qal Perfect 3ms. Shift to the Perfect aspect indicates a fixed, completed state of naming.
grammatical-notes.אֶחָד: Adjective/Number. One (cardinal). Signals a unique and unified totality.

dictionary-meaning.וַיִּקְרָא: Then Elohim assigned the functional name of — disclosing the sovereign and legal act of defining identity (Rule 24).
dictionary-meaning.קָרָא: He had already assigned the name of — disclosing the shift to the Perfect aspect indicating a completed assignment (Rule 9).
dictionary-meaning.עֶרֶב: Evening (the period of mixing and transition) — disclosing the functional state of obscured boundaries (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.בֹּקֶר: Morning (the period of discernment and clarity) — disclosing the functional state of returning order (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.אֶחָד: Day one (one unique and unified day) — disclosing the cardinal 'One' (Echad) which signifies the unification of the period (Rule 69).

קרא.reason: Naming in the Hebrew creation context is an act of sovereign dominion and legal definition.

main-translation: ***Then Elohim assigned the functional name of 'Day' to the light, and to the darkness He had already assigned the name of 'Night'***; ***and there was evening (the period of mixing and transition) and there was morning (the period of discernment and clarity), comprising day one (one unique and unified day).***

main-translation-explanation.Then Elohim assigned the functional name: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Discloses the legal dominion and functional definition inherent in naming (Rule 24).
main-translation-explanation.He had already assigned the name of: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Discloses the shift to the Perfect aspect 'Qara', indicating a fixed, completed assignment (Rule 9).
main-translation-explanation.evening (mixing) / morning (discernment): Discloses the functional roots of the words to show the author's logic of visibility (Rule 21).
main-translation-explanation.comprising day one: Restored the cardinal 'One' (Echad) to disclose the unique and unified nature of the day (Rule 69).

rejected-translations.reason: Uses an ordinal number ('first') when the text uses a cardinal number ('one'), masking the uniqueness of the day.
Too thin; fails to disclose the legal act of defining function (Rule 24).
